{{Quote|C'mon old timer, what're you trying to hand me? Everybody knows that Seoul 5 was destroyed in a cosmic war eons ago.|Han Solo|<ref name="marvel84" />}}
'''Seoul 5''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] which was home to an ancient [[Human/Legends|Human]] civilization in the [[Pre-Republic era]]. The [[Seoularian]]s, as they were called, powered their cities with enormously powerful [[Seoularian crystal|crystals]] that were kept in large supplies underground. Seoul 5 was long uninhabited by the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], and its location lost—however, the legendary lost world was finally rediscovered in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Description==
{{Quote|I warped back into normal space in an uncharted sector, near an unknown solar system... and set down for repairs on the fifth planet, which my instruments showed to be habitable.|A former Imperial scout details landing on Seoul 5|<ref name="marvel84" />}}
Located deep within [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], Seoul 5 was a heavily [[Forest/Legends|forested]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] easily inhabited by [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="marvel84" />

==History==
{{Quote|I'm ''afraid'' the doctor and I discovered the charts that led the Imperials you're hiding from to Seoul 5, ''Solo''. The Empire backed our expedition and sent along these ''geeks'' to 'protect' us. Things went well until we found those crystals out there. According to the inscriptions on the base of the pedestal, the Seoularians used the crystals to mentally power ''every'' device on the planet.|Fem Nu-Ar|<ref name="marvel84" />}}
[[File:Onraisquare.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A statue to the goddess Onrai in a square on Seoul 5]]
Seoul 5 was once home to a civilization of early Humans who were said to have left the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Notron]]—"the cradle of Humans in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]"—to settle the world. The Seoularian civilization eventually expanded beyond the bounds of Seoul 5, colonizing a number of outpost worlds,<ref name="marvel84" /> and fell under the yoke of the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] at some point during the [[Pre-Republic era]].<ref name="atlas" /> Seoul 5 eventually became little more than a legend after its society declined, its location lost and the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] itself commonly believed to have been destroyed in a [[Unidentified cosmic war|cosmic war]]. However, Seoul 5 was finally rediscovered during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Shortly before the outbreak of open warfare between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], a lone Imperial scout stumbled upon Seoul 5 after his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was knocked off course while entering [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The scout explored the intact remains of a large, ruined city and discovered a large cache of [[Seoularian crystal]]s. The visitor left upon repairing his craft, taking a crystal as a keepsake, but a planned return voyage was scuttled by the outbreak of war.<ref name="marvel84" />

That crystal, along with Seoul 5's location, fell into the hands of Rebel hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], not long after the Empire's climactic [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Solo arrived on Seoul 5 to find that he wasn't the only one to rediscover the world—he had been preceded by an Imperial landing party led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Drezzel]], who followed research by [[Xenoarchaeology/Legends|xenoarchaeologists]] [[Ualp Xathan]] and [[Fem Nu-Ar]] to the lost planet. Solo and the Imperials came to [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire as both parties contested a valuable supply of Seoularian crystals, and Solo ultimately overloaded the city's [[crystal control room]] in order to wipe out the Imperial force. Solo narrowly escaped the city along with Xathan and Nu-Ar, and left the world uninhabited once again.<ref name="marvel84" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|This is the mother goddess ''Onrai'', she who gives and never receives, mistress of the planet ''Notron'', cradle of all Humans in the galaxy. If you would learn more, touch ''Onrai'', and doors will open.|Inscription on a statue of the goddess Onrai on Seoul 5|<ref name="marvel84" />}}
Seoul 5's ancient Human civilization was long-vanished by the planet's rediscovery in 4 ABY, but left echoes of its passing both on Seoul 5 and its colony worlds. The people of Seoul 5, who spoke a language called [[Seoulian]], worshipped the mother [[God/Legends|goddess]] [[Onrai]], placing large statues of the deity in their cities.<ref name="marvel84" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Chewie, look at those ruins! This has gotta be it!|Han Solo|<ref name="marvel84" />}}
Seoul 5 was denoted by massive cities left behind by the Seoularian civilization, some of which were remarkably well-preserved by the Galactic Civil War, many generations after Seoul 5 was depopulated. At least one of these featured a prominent statue of the goddess Onrai in the city center. The cities were powered by unique radiant crystals, which were kept in chambers and tunnels deep underground.<ref name="marvel84" />

==Behind the scenes==
Seoul 5 appeared as the primary setting of [[Star Wars (1977) 84|''Star Wars'' (1977) 84]], written by [[Roy Richardson]], illustrated by [[David Mazzucchelli]] and released in [[1984]].<ref name="marvel84" />
